title: 6ml Porcelain Pepper Mill Mortar Pestle Pugging Pot Garlic Spice Grinder Pharmacy Herbs Bowl Mill Crusher DIY Kitchen Gadget
description:
About this item
Perfect for grinding species, herbs, medicine, or other substance
Volume: 6ml;Material: Porcelain;Color: White.
The mortar comes with a flat and unglazed bottom for stability.
Mortar Diameter: Approx. 2.3 inch outside diameter, 1.8 inch inside diameter.
Pestle Size: Approx. 2.6 inch long, 0.4 inch diameter (smaller end), 0.8 inch diameter (larger end).
reviews_5s:
It is a tiny mortar and pestle and I bought it to pulverize my Mameres meds for end of life care. She could no longer swallow pills or solids so I would crush them in this handy item and mix them in applesauce! She just passed a few days ago and I am going through her items. I had to write a review for this item as it was such a tiny, yet big, help in aiding her hospice days. I had also picked a "pill crusher" device not having experience in this type of care and never reached for it as this mortar and pestle so finely milled the meds I need not try anything else! It's affordable, durable,
and quite handy! It fits in your hand easily so if you are needing big batches to mill - look elsewhere. Thank you for taking time to read my review! I hope you have a great rest of your day.We purchased this to grind niacin tablets to add to our duckling's water. The pestle is small but still fits nicely in your hand without making your hand cramp. We accidentally dropped the pestal on the floor a couple times and it survived the fall without breaking. The mortar is a good size for pill grinding or grinding an individual spice for a recipe. Any quantity of spices greater than about a tablespoon would probably be too much for its capacity.I never thought I'd have a need for one of these, but I found myself faced with a recipe that needed ground saffron. I took a chance on the smallest and cheapest mortar and pestle on Amazon and am very pleased with this one. The weight of both is just enough to make grinding very easy, yet the size is small enough to fit into your cabinet without occupying too much space. This is perfect for those who might need to grind small pills or the occasional dried herb/spice.

10/10 recommend!Play Video
My new Koi betta wasn't eating the new pellets I got from Amazon. The pellets seem to have the healthiest ingredients, all natural betta fish foods, that I could find. Then, I realized that although the pellets are extremely small, they're still too big for him.

I ordered the mortar and pestle just in case I needed to break up foods for him, and I needed it! As you can see, Iightly broke the pellet into smaller pieces, which he eats immediately.

It's a good size and I like that it's unfinished on the inside of the mortar and the end of the pestle, so the pellets don't slide around.I have to take horse pills every morning & I can't swallow them. I was told to break them in half but even then it's hard to swallow so I decided to crush them & got this little mortar & pestle. I have to admit it's smaller than I expected but it does the job great. It really is the right size for crushing a big pill. I love it.I bought this to further mash up walnuts after grinding in my regular blender, because just had most of my bottom molar teeth removed and wanted to make sure all were totally smashed up. Well, this worked wonders as the cheap inextenepentive product I was needing for a short term project until my bottom gums heal and I can get dentures to replace my missing botten teeth. I rely on walnuts as a low choleseail l meat sourceI read reviews about how it is not glazed on the inside or the bottom of the lower end of the pestle. These parts are ruff. Well they were right. I am pretty sure that was intentional. The glaze would probably have cracked and chipped off during use. If you are using the little mortar for grinding salt or herbs then you would be consuming salt and glaze. The inside of the mortar and the lower end of the pestle are unglazed and ruff. A piece of 200 grit sandpaper will smooth it up to your desired level of smooth.
Some reviews I read said the mortar and pestle are small. Well yep they are. If you want a small mortar for grinding little bit of pink salt into powder, or grind pepper into dust, this set is perfect. Don't think that you are going to buy this and start an herb grinding business. Not going to make it. If you want to grind up a little kosher salt or pink Himalayan salt to dust for putting on popcorn this is the the unit. I am happy with this mortar and pestle and I would recommend it to a friend.I thought it was a bit bigger but that’s my fault for not reading it. But I have used it and it works great for what I needed. It wouldn’t be for lots of stuff being crushed at once. It’s very durable and strongSturdy, well made, does its job. My only complaint is that I didn't expect it to be quite as porus as it is. Probably my fault for an unreasonable expectation. Still, can't fault it. It is what it is. So far no cracks, it's held up well. Arrived quickly.I didn't realize it was this small. That was my fault for not reading the item before the purchase. It serves its purpose for the time being. Thanks
reviews_1s:
Could have done better using a hammer. Didn't crush pills wellThis is like if an amateur took a ceramics class. I cannot even describe my astonishment, it came all unsymmetric and unglazed. This product is a complete joke be warned this many good reviews for a product this horrible is extremely suspicious do NOT buy.I wanted this item to grind medication. The outside is glazed, the inside is not glazed. Unfortunately, glaze was allowed to drop inside the bowl, forming uneven surfaces and sharp points. I’m afraid I’d get that material in my pill powder if I tried to use it.The Pestal has a ridge molded-in right on the grinding surface (see picture) and only only the top inch is ceramic coated. The mortar (the bowl) has an imperfection in the grinding surface, and the ceramic coating does not cover the sides of the bowl.
Overall very cheep and does not perform very well due to defects.Only parts of the item are smooth it's like they only glazed some of it and didn't put it in a kiln. Don't recommend to anyoneThe photos make it seem like the the inside of the mortar is glazed. This was the only appeal for me. Neither the inside of the mortar nor bottom of the pestle club is glazed. Only the outside of the mortar and top of the pestle. Pretty useless.Super small and cheaply madeUsed for grinding pills. Broke after 2 usages not good quality!Was expecting it to be bigger!This is not porcelain like I expected. It is rough, not smooth like I had before. Very different. I am definitely disappointed in the quality of this. Both pieces are rough. Not smooth porcelain as expected. It would be a pain to try and send it back so I will keep the piece of junk.
